summary: jupiter-sdk executes remote code fetched from IPFS on import
details: On `import jupiter_sdk`, the package's top-level __init__.py fetches a payload over HTTPS from a public IPFS gateway (https://gateway.pinata.cloud/ipfs/QmP2RrfNCNabLzPYncMGgFwAmttDafczpJLS8oxvQRBVqg), passes the response body directly to exec(), and invokes a main() function from the executed namespace, with errors silently swallowed. The URL is content-addressed to an IPFS CID with no signature or hash verification of the executed content beyond the CID, and the fetched code is not shipped with the package. The package presents itself as a 'Jupiter DEX Aggregator SDK' but its exported API (get_quote, get_token_list, get_routes) returns hardcoded dummy values and performs no real Jupiter calls, so the only functional behavior on install/import is the remote code loader. The name resembles the widely used Jupiter aggregator ecosystem while performing arbitrary remote code execution against any environment that imports it.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
